site.title: Practical Prompt Engineering
site.subtitle: A local-first playground for learning and experimenting with prompt engineering techniques.
site.localStorageNote: Every demo on this site stores data only in your browser's localStorage. Nothing is sent to a server or the cloud. Clearing your browser storage will permanently delete saved prompts, so use Export (where available) to back up your work.

sections.name: Zero-Shot Prompt
sections.summary: Baseline prompt library: author prompts, save them locally, render them as cards, and delete them.
sections.technique: Zero-shot prompting asks the model to perform a task without any examples. This folder is the simplest save/browse experience used as the reference baseline.

sections.name: One-Shot Prompt
sections.summary: Single-example prompting variation explored through a small UI.
sections.technique: One-shot prompting provides a single example before the real task, guiding the model's output format and style.

sections.name: Few-Shot Prompt
sections.summary: Few-shot prompting variation (multiple examples) in a UI-friendly way.
sections.technique: Few-shot prompting supplies several examples to establish a pattern the model can generalize from.

sections.name: Standard Prompt
sections.summary: A direct question or instruction — the simplest, foundational form of prompting.
sections.technique: A standard prompt is a direct question or instruction to an AI. It is the foundation that all other prompting techniques build upon.

sections.name: Structured Output
sections.summary: Prompt library with metadata tracking, token estimation, confidence coloring, and timestamps.
sections.technique: Structured output prompting asks the model to return results in a fixed schema (JSON, etc.) so responses are machine-parseable.

sections.name: Zero-Shot Chain-of-Thought
sections.summary: Chain-of-thought exploration plus import/export conflict-resolution implementation notes.
sections.technique: Zero-shot CoT elicits step-by-step reasoning (e.g. 'think step by step') without providing worked examples.

sections.name: Personas
sections.summary: Persona-driven prompting (tone/role shaping) with technical and UX specs.
sections.technique: Persona prompting assigns the model a role or identity to shape tone, expertise, and response style.

sections.name: Delimiters & XML
sections.summary: Prompt organization, delimiters/XML tagging, and prompt-management architecture research.
sections.technique: Delimiters and structured tags separate instructions from content, improving reliability and reducing prompt-injection risk.

sections.name: Emotional Prompt
sections.technique: Emotional prompting adds affective language ('this is important for my career') to nudge more careful model responses.
